name: Burriana and Marjal de Nules Coastal Loop
name_original: Desde Nules hasta Burriana – Circular al Estany de Nules
description: Enjoy an easy 15.7-mile (25.3 km) touring cycle from Nules to Burriana, circling Estany de Nules with minimal elevation gain and birdwatchin
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nules offer a diverse landscape for cyclists, ranging from coastal plains to the foothills of the Serra d'Espadà mountain range. The region features a mix of rural agricultural areas, particularly orange groves, and more elevated terrain with varied gradients. This area provides a network of paths suitable for different cycling preferences, including routes near the Mediterranean coast and those venturing inland. Best touring cycling routes around Nules The most popular touring cycling route is From Nules to Burriana – Circular to Estany de Nules, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the Nules Pond area. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mascarell Walled Town – Basilica of Our Lady of Lledó loop from Nules - La Vilavella, a moderate 36.2 miles (58.2 km) path. This route passes through the historic Mascarell Walled Town and includes cultural landmarks. Local touring cyclists also love the Mascarell Walled Town loop from Nules - La Vilavella, a 35.3 miles (56.9 km) trail leading through the historic Mascarell Walled Town, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Touring cycling around Nules is defined by coastal paths, agricultural fields, and the varied terrain of the Serra d'Espadà foothills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Nules are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nules's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nules?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes around Nules, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nules?
A: Nules offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists, ranging from flat coastal plains and rural agricultural areas, particularly orange groves, to the more elevated and varied terrain of the Serra d'Espadà mountain range foothills. You'll find a mix of coastal paths, agricultural fields, and routes venturing inland.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Nules?
A: Yes, Nules offers several easy touring cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938374416/from-nules-to-burriana-circular-to-estany-de-nules" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Nules to Burriana – Circular to Estany de Nules&lt;/a&gt; route is an easy 15.7-mile (25.3 km) trail that explores the Nules Pond area, ideal for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Nules?
A: For more experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Nules offers difficult routes that venture into the Serra d'Espadà foothills. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38265309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mascarell Walled Town – Viewpoint of les Penyes Altes loop from Nules - La Vilavella&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 46.9 km with significant elevation gain, offering rewarding views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Nules?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Nules are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10386026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mascarell Walled Town – Basilica of Our Lady of Lledó loop from Nules - La Vilavella&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular route that takes you through historic areas and cultural landmarks.

Q: What cultural or historical landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: You can explore several interesting landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34428357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mascarell Walled Town loop from Nules - La Vilavella&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the 'magical and completely walled village' of Mascarell. Additionally, the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2756864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Castle&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking mountain views as a reward for the climb.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I discover while cycling in Nules?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4104060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nules Pond&lt;/a&gt;, explore routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the challenging yet scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt; within the Serra d'Espadà, known for its spectacular panoramic views.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through orange groves?
A: Yes, the rural agricultural areas around Nules are characterized by extensive orange groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33810070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nules Pond – Orange groves near Burriana loop from Moncofa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically highlights these picturesque agricultural fields.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nules?
A: Nules offers good cycling conditions for much of the year due to its Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still enjoying mild weather.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nules?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling in Nules, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscape, which includes coastal paths, agricultural fields, and the varied terrain of the Serra d'Espadà foothills, offering options for all difficulty levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Nules?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of 10 easy routes, such as the one exploring the Nules Pond area, suggests that there are options suitable for families looking for less strenuous rides on relatively flat terrain.
tips: Very quiet beach and not so crowded. On one side of the Clot you have sand (in this case) and on the other side to the north it is made of stone so you can choose according to your tastes!
Interesting "estany" that houses different poultry fauna, ducks, cormorants, etc. With benches and shade to rest for a while.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the 'From Nules to Burriana – Circular to Estany de Nules' route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is generally easy, consisting mostly of paved surfaces such as cycleways, streets, and roads. There are some unpaved sections, but the overall experience is smooth and suitable for touring bicycles.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered easy with a minimal elevation gain of approximately 40 meters over its 25.3-kilometer length. This makes it suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families looking for a relaxed cycling experience.

faqs.question: What natural features or wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse natural beauty. A significant highlight is the Nules Pond (Estany de Nules), which is home to various bird species like ducks and cormorants. The route may also feature picturesque orange groves near Burriana.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the Mediterranean climate of the region, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. These seasons avoid the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter, providing ideal conditions for enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this circular route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, you can start and end in the same location. The starting point is easily accessible via public transport, suggesting good access points within Nules or Burriana. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, look for public parking in Nules, especially near the town center or close to the Estany de Nules area.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself is generally suitable for cycling with dogs in a trailer or basket, given its easy terrain. However, when visiting natural areas like the Nules Pond, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially the bird species present.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling through the Marjal de Nules area?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the Marjal de Nules. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public paths within this natural park. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations regarding protected areas, such as staying on marked paths and not disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Castellón de la Plana - Nules route (33%) and also includes sections of El Camí IT5TR8 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana (7%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there any good spots for a break or to rest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around the Nules Pond (Estany de Nules) provides benches and shade, offering pleasant spots for a break and to observe the birdlife.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights along the 'From Nules to Burriana – Circular to Estany de Nules' route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlights along this route include the serene Nules Pond (Estany de Nules), known for its bird species, and the scenic Arenal Beach. You'll also pass through picturesque orange groves.

faqs.question: How accessible is the starting point of the route by public transport?
faqs.answer: The route is recognized for its accessibility, with its starting point being easily reachable via public transport. This makes it convenient for cyclists who prefer not to drive to the trailhead.
